revision.text:
'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう)は[[近江国]]東部(琵琶湖東岸)を支配した[[国造]]。淡海安国造・近淡海安国造とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。
=== 氏族 === [[安氏]]。[[姓]]は[[直]]。
=== 本拠 === 近江国[[野洲郡]]。現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。
=== 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]] …… [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。
=== 子孫 === *[[大陀牟夜別]] …… 淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 *[[犬上御田鍬]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|やすのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:近江国|やすのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史|やすのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:古墳時代|やすのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代|やすのくにのみやつこ]]

== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。

=== 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]] …… [[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。
=== 子孫 === *[[大陀牟夜別]] …… 淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 *[[犬上御田鍬]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。
=== 氏族 === [[安氏]]。[[姓]]は[[直]]。
=== 本拠 === 近江国[[野洲郡]]（現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]）。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。
=== 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]]・・・[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。
=== 子孫 === * [[大陀牟夜別]]・・・淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 * [[犬上御田鍬]]・・・[[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。 == 概要 == === 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。 === 氏族 === [[安氏]]。[[姓]]は[[直]]。 === 本拠 === 近江国[[野洲郡]]（現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]）。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。 === 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]]・・・[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。 === 子孫 === * [[大陀牟夜別]]・・・淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 * [[犬上御田鍬]]・・・[[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。 == 誤植 == 御上祝（みかみのはふり、[[御上神社]][[社家|神職家系]]）は[[彦坐王]]の後裔、安国造（[[安氏|近淡海之安直]]）の一族という話があるが、これは誤植である。
厳密には、安国造の祖、[[水之穂真若王]]（みずのほまわかのみこ）の母親（[[息長水依比売命]]｛おきながのみずよりひめのみこと｝、[[三上氏]]から[[息長氏]]に養女に出された）の出身家系、[[三上氏]]が御上祝であり、その後世まで三上氏が御上祝を続ける。御上祝（三上氏）は[[神武天皇|磐余彦]]（いわれひこ、即位以前の[[神武天皇]]の名前または、本名）が生まれる以前からの歴史があり、[[山背国造|山背国造（山背氏・山代氏）]]や[[凡河内国造|凡河内国造（凡河内氏・大河内氏・広峯氏）]]などを[[庶流]]として排出した[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]（[[天照大神]]の3男）家系の[[嫡流]]の[[神別|神別氏族]][[天孫族]]（天照大神の後裔且つ[[皇別|皇別氏族]]｛[[皇室]]から[[臣籍降下]]で身分を落とした家系｝を除く家系で、[[天孫族]]と[[天神族]]がいる[[天津神・国津神|天津神]]｛[[天孫降臨]]した神々｝の中でも高貴とされる家系）である。天孫族の嫡流は三上氏の他に、[[天之忍穂耳命]]の嫡流である[[皇室]]と、[[天穂日命]]の嫡流の[[出雲国造]]の3系統がおり、身分上、最上位とされる[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/816099 「御上神社沿革考 : 近江国野洲郡三上村鎮座」 大谷治作 編 / 出版: 太田治左衛門 / 出版年月日: 明32.2]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。[[皇別|皇別氏族]]は神武天皇以降に[[皇室]]から別れた家系であり、安国造も[[皇別]]である。神武天皇以前は皇室家系と同じく[[天孫族]]である詳細は各リンクを参照のこと。。
なお、三上氏は天津彦根命の御子神（一人息子）の[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命]][[天火明命]]と[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]は、同一神と言われている。の[[天孫降臨]]時に[[随伴]]した32神のうちの1神）が[[近江国]][[野洲郡]]三上郷の[[三上山]]（旧称・御影山）に天孫降臨したことにはじまり、御上祝とは、天之御影命の御孫神、[[彦伊賀都命]]（ひこいがつのみこと、第一子且つ長男）が[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]を[[御上神社]]の前身として、三上山で[[古神道]]様式で祀ったことにはじまる。[[山背国造|山背国造（山背氏・山代氏）]]・[[凡河内国造|凡河内国造（凡河内氏・大河内氏・広峯氏）]]などは、この彦伊賀都命の弟神（次男・[[阿多根命]]｛山背国造家系｝、三男・[[彦己曽根命]]｛凡河内国造家系｝）から別れた庶流氏族である。また続柄上、彦伊賀都命の御子神、[[天夷沙比止命]]と[[神武天皇|磐余彦（神武天皇）]]が同位となる[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991097/118 天神本記（先代旧事本記巻第三）P208-228（コマ番号118-128）/国史大系. 第7巻]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション。 == 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店） {{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。 == 概要 == === 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。 === 氏族 === [[安氏]]。[[姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]。 === 本拠 === 近江国[[野洲郡]]（現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]）。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。 === 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]]・・・[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。 === 子孫 === * [[大陀牟夜別]]・・・淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 * [[犬上御田鍬]]・・・[[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。 == 誤植 == 御上祝（みかみのはふり、[[御上神社]][[社家|神職家系]]）は[[彦坐王]]の後裔、安国造（[[安氏|近淡海之安直]]）の一族という話があるが、これは誤植である。
厳密には、安国造の祖、[[水之穂真若王]]（みずのほまわかのみこ）の母親（[[息長水依比売命]]｛おきながのみずよりひめのみこと｝、[[三上氏]]から[[息長氏]]に養女に出された）の出身家系、[[三上氏]]が御上祝であり、その後世まで三上氏が御上祝を続ける。御上祝（三上氏）は[[神武天皇|磐余彦]]（いわれひこ、即位以前の[[神武天皇]]の名前または、本名）が生まれる以前からの歴史があり、[[山背国造|山背国造（山背氏・山代氏）]]や[[凡河内国造|凡河内国造（凡河内氏・大河内氏・広峯氏）]]などを[[庶流]]として排出した[[アマツヒコネ|天津彦根命]]（[[天照大神]]の3男）家系の[[嫡流]]の[[神別|神別氏族]][[天孫族]]（天照大神の後裔且つ[[皇別|皇別氏族]]｛[[皇室]]から[[臣籍降下]]で身分を落とした家系｝を除く家系で、[[天孫族]]と[[天神族]]がいる[[天津神・国津神|天津神]]｛[[天孫降臨]]した神々｝の中でも高貴とされる家系）である。天孫族の嫡流は三上氏の他に、[[天之忍穂耳命]]の嫡流である[[皇室]]と、[[天穂日命]]の嫡流の[[出雲国造]]の3系統がおり、身分上、最上位とされる[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/10301765/84 御上祝家系図（三上氏）（コマ番号84-97）/諸系譜. 第28冊]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/816099 「御上神社沿革考 : 近江国野洲郡三上村鎮座」 大谷治作 編 / 出版: 太田治左衛門 / 出版年月日: 明32.2]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）。[[皇別|皇別氏族]]は神武天皇以降に[[皇室]]から別れた家系であり、安国造も[[皇別]]である。神武天皇以前は皇室家系と同じく[[天孫族]]である詳細は各リンクを参照のこと。。
なお、三上氏は天津彦根命の御子神（一人息子）の[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]（[[ニギハヤヒ|天照国照彦火明櫛玉饒速日命]][[天火明命]]と[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]は、同一神と言われている。の[[天孫降臨]]時に[[随伴]]した32神のうちの1神）が[[近江国]][[野洲郡]]三上郷の[[三上山]]（旧称・御影山）に天孫降臨したことにはじまり、御上祝とは、天之御影命の御孫神、[[彦伊賀都命]]（ひこいがつのみこと、第一子且つ長男）が[[天目一箇神|天之御影命]]を[[御上神社]]の前身として、三上山で[[古神道]]様式で祀ったことにはじまる。[[山背国造|山背国造（山背氏・山代氏）]]・[[凡河内国造|凡河内国造（凡河内氏・大河内氏・広峯氏）]]などは、この彦伊賀都命の弟神（次男・[[阿多根命]]｛山背国造家系｝、三男・[[彦己曽根命]]｛凡河内国造家系｝）から別れた庶流氏族である。また続柄上、彦伊賀都命の御子神、[[天夷沙比止命]]と[[神武天皇|磐余彦（神武天皇）]]が同位となる[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991097/118 天神本記（先代旧事本記巻第三）P208-228（コマ番号118-128）/国史大系. 第7巻]　-　国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション。 == 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店） {{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王|彦坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系。
=== 氏族 === [[安氏]]。[[姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]。
=== 本拠 === 近江国[[野洲郡]]（現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]）。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。
=== 氏神 === [[建部神社|建部大社]]・・・[[滋賀県]][[大津市]]。祭神は[[日本武尊]]。近江国一宮。
=== 子孫 === * [[大陀牟夜別]]・・・淡海国造。安国造の祖。彦坐王3世孫。娘の[[両道入姫|両道入姫命]]は日本武尊に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 * [[犬上御田鍬]]・・・[[飛鳥時代]]の外交官。[[遣隋使]]。初代[[遣唐使]]。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === *『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王]]の子の[[水穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系とされる。 *一方、水穂真若命を祖とする蒲生稲置、菅田首は[[天津彦根命]]の後裔とされており、実際には水穂真若命が[[国忍富命]]の子に置かれるものと見られる<ref name="houga">[[宝賀寿男]]「第2章 天孫系氏族 第2節 三上氏族 1三上祝、三上直」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、1067-1069頁。</ref>。
=== 氏族 === '''[[安氏]]'''（やすうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。
=== 本拠 === [[近江国]][[野洲郡]]で、現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]に相当する。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう。
=== 氏神 === 不明。
=== 人物 === *[[大陀牟夜別君]]（おおたむやわけのきみ、大多牟阪王/意富多牟和気） *:初代淡海安国造。[[彦坐王]]3世孫または[[天津彦根命]]の後裔。 *[[布多遅比売]]（ふたぢひめ、両道入姫命） *:大陀牟夜別君の娘。[[倭建命]]に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 *坂田垂根君（さかたのたりねのきみ） *:大陀牟夜別君の子。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]

== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王]]の子の[[水之穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系とされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[安氏]]'''（やすうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。
=== 本拠 === [[近江国]][[野洲郡]]で、現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]に相当する。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
=== 氏神 === 不明。[[天之御影神]]とも<ref>[[川口謙二]]『東京美術選書23続神々の系図』（[[東京美術]]、1980年）p.66.</ref>。
=== 人物 === *[[大多牟坂王]]（おおたむさかのみこ、大陀牟夜別/意富多牟和気） *:初代淡海安国造。[[彦坐王]]三世孫または[[天津彦根命]]の後裔。 *[[布多遅比売]]（ふたぢひめ、両道入姫命） *:大多牟坂王の娘。[[倭建命]]に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 *坂田垂根君（さかたのたりねのきみ） *:大多牟坂王の子。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District|ja|野洲郡}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} and {{ill|Moriyama|ja|守山市}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) * The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) * Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) * Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
[[Category:Stubs related to Japanese history]] [[Category:Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Shiga Prefecture]] [[ja:安国造]]

'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District|ja|野洲郡}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} and {{ill|Moriyama|ja|守山市}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) * The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) * Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) * Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)

He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) * Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) * Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto.

'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District|ja|野洲郡}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} and {{ill|Moriyama|ja|守山市}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) * The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) * Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) * Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)

'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District|ja|野洲郡}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu|ja|野洲市}} and {{ill|Moriyama|ja|守山市}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) * The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) * Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) * Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)

revision.text:
'''安国造'''（やすのくにのみやつこ・やすこくぞう）は[[近江国]]東部（[[琵琶湖]]東岸）を支配した[[国造]]。'''淡海安国造'''、'''近淡海安国造'''とも。
== 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[古事記]]』によると祖は[[日子坐王]]の子の[[水之穂真若王]]で、[[淡海国造]]と同系とされる。
=== 氏族 === '''[[安氏]]'''（やすうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。
=== 本拠 === [[近江国]][[野洲郡]]で、現在の[[滋賀県]][[野洲市]]および[[守山市]]に相当する。後に[[淡海国造]]の支配下に置かれたともいう{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}。
=== 氏神 === 不明。[[天之御影神]]とも<ref>[[川口謙二]]『東京美術選書23続神々の系図』（[[東京美術]]、1980年）p.66.</ref>。
=== 人物 === *[[大多牟坂王]]（おおたむさかのみこ、大陀牟夜別/意富多牟和気） *:初代淡海安国造。[[彦坐王]]三世孫または[[天津彦根命]]の後裔。 *[[布多遅比売]]（ふたぢひめ、両道入姫命） *:大多牟坂王の娘。[[倭建命]]に嫁いで[[稲依別王]]を産んだ。 *坂田垂根君（さかたのたりねのきみ） *:大多牟坂王の子。
== 関連項目 == * [[国造の一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:やすのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:近江国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:滋賀県の歴史]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11450454]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] along the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|qq=|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District]] in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]] and [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
{{Mikami Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|安国造|version=99619397|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].

=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11450454]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] along the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District]] in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]] and [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
{{Mikami Faith}}

He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11450454]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] along the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District]] in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]] and [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
{{Mikami Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|安国造|version=99619397|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==

Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>

She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450454]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no Miyatsuko]], or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]] along the eastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from [[:en:Yasu District, Shiga|Yasu District]] in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern [[:en:Yasu, Shiga|Yasu]] and [[:en:Moriyama, Shiga|Moriyama]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
{{Mikami Faith}}

=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11450454]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}} and {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|en|Moriyama, Shiga|WD=Q857225|lt=Moriyama}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)

She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]]
'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Yasu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu}} and {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|en|Moriyama, Shiga|WD=Q857225|lt=Moriyama}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== Notes ==
{{reflist}}
== References == * *Dictionary of National History* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * Tarō Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, *Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names* (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan) * *Japanese Historical Dictionary* (Yamakawa Shuppansha) * *Shintō Dictionary* (Rinsen Shoten)
{{Mikami Faith}}
{{translated page|ja|安国造|version=99619397|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:安国造]]
'''Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko''' (安国造) was a {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kuni no miyatsuko}}, or regional ruler, who governed the eastern part of {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}} along the eastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. The title is also referred to as "Ōmi Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (淡海安国造) or "Kinan Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko" (近淡海安国造).
== Overview == === Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko's ancestor was {{ill|Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizunohowake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135578859|1=Mizuho no Mawaka no Mikoto}}, a son of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|日子坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦坐王}}, and is thus considered related to the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11559784|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}}.
=== Clan === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko belonged to the '''{{ill|Yasu clan|ja|安氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasu clan|1=Q28684171|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28684171}}''' (Yasu-uji), whose {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} was '''{{ill|Ason|ja|直 (姓)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ason|qq=}}'''.
=== Base of Operations === The Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko governed from {{ill|Yasu District, Shiga|en|Yasu District, Shiga|WD=Q8049933|lt=Yasu District|1=Yasu District, Shiga}} in Ōmi Province, corresponding to modern {{ill|Yasu, Shiga|en|Yasu, Shiga|WD=Q611642|lt=Yasu|1=Yasu, Shiga}} and {{ill|Moriyama, Shiga|en|Moriyama, Shiga|WD=Q857225|lt=Moriyama|1=Moriyama, Shiga}} in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. Some sources suggest they later came under the control of the {{ill|Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|淡海国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-no-Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Q11559784|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11559784}} [citation needed].
=== Clan Deity === The clan's {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} is unknown, but {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|qq=}} has been suggested.<ref>Kenji Kawaguchi, *Tokyo Bijutsu Selection 23: Continuation of the Genealogies of the Gods* (Tokyo Bijutsu, 1980), p. 66.</ref>
=== Notable Figures === * '''Ōtamusaka no Mikoto''' (大多牟坂王, also known as Ōtamuya Wake or Ōtamuwake) ** The first Ankoku-no-Miyatsuko of Ōmi. He was a third-generation descendant of {{ill|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|ja|彦坐王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto|qq=}} or a descendant of {{ill|Amatsuhikone|ja|天津彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsuhikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマツヒコネ}}. * '''Futaji Hime''' (布多遅比売, also known as Ryōdōiri Hime) ** Daughter of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. She married {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} and bore {{ill|Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja|稲依別王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaiwawake no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Sakata Tarine no Kimi''' (坂田垂根君) ** Son of Ōtamusaka no Mikoto. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
